message:
docs(tracking): Mark async error fixtures and manifest complete

Updates async-error-coverage-todo.md to reflect completion of all fixture
and manifest test case work across 7 languages. This milestone represents
a major step toward production-ready async error path coverage in UBS.

Progress Summary:
=================

Fixtures & Tests Section (all items now complete):
- ✅ Add failing/clean fixtures for JS (2 files: buggy + clean)
- ✅ Add failing/clean fixtures for Python (2 files: buggy + clean)
- ✅ Add failing/clean fixtures for Go (2 files: buggy + clean)
- ✅ Add failing/clean fixtures for C++ (2 files: buggy + clean)
- ✅ Add failing/clean fixtures for Rust (2 files: buggy + clean)
- ✅ Add failing/clean fixtures for Java (2 files: buggy + clean)
- ✅ Add failing/clean fixtures for Ruby (2 files: buggy + clean)
- ✅ Update test-suite/manifest.json (14 test cases: 7 languages × 2 each)

Total Deliverables:
- 14 fixture files (7 buggy + 7 clean)
- 14 manifest test cases (7 buggy validation + 7 clean baseline)
- 336 lines added to manifest.json
- ~300 lines of fixture code demonstrating async patterns

Implementation Status Recap:
=============================

From Research & Planning through Implementation Tracking:

Completed (6/7 languages):
1. ✅ JavaScript: 3 rules implemented (await-no-try, then-no-catch, promiseall-no-try)
2. ✅ Python: 2 rules implemented (await-no-try, task-no-await)
3. ✅ Go: 3 rules implemented (goroutine error handling patterns)
4. ✅ C++: 3 rules implemented (async-no-try, future-never-accessed, thread-exceptions)
5. ✅ Java: 2 rules implemented (future-get-no-try, then-no-exceptionally)
6. ✅ Rust: 2 rules implemented (await-no-handler, tokio-task-no-await)

Partial (1/7 languages):
7. ⏳ Ruby: Metadata defined, run_async_error_checks() pending implementation

Fixture/Test Coverage: 7/7 languages complete (including Ruby!)
Scanner Coverage: 6/7 languages complete (Ruby pending)

Remaining Work:
===============

Per Documentation / Handoff section:
- [ ] Run targeted UBS manifest(s) to validate new checks fire
- [ ] Capture validation results and warning counts in notes/issue
- [ ] Update TODO status in final summary
- [ ] Close issue ultimate_bug_scanner-e3j once all work complete

Next Immediate Steps:
=====================

1. **Validation Run**:
   ```bash
   ./test-suite/run_manifest.py --filter async-errors --verbose
   ```
   Expected: 14/14 tests pass with correct warning counts

2. **Document Results**:
   Add results to issue ultimate_bug_scanner-e3j notes:
   - JS: 3/3 rules fire on buggy, 0 warnings on clean ✓
   - Python: 2/2 rules fire on buggy, 0 warnings on clean ✓
   - ... (document all 7 languages)

3. **Complete Ruby Implementation**:
   Implement run_async_error_checks() in modules/ubs-ruby.sh:
   - Use ast-grep rule for Thread.new blocks without rescue
   - Follow same pattern as other language modules
   - Test with ruby/buggy/async_errors.rb fixture

4. **CI Integration** (blocked):
   Issue ultimate_bug_scanner-o3l (CI integration) is blocked by
   ultimate_bug_scanner-6ig (resource lifecycle bug). Once #6ig is fixed,
   manifest runner can be added to CI pipeline.

Impact:
=======

This update marks the transition from "implementation phase" to "validation phase"
for async error coverage. All test infrastructure is now in place:

- Detection rules: Implemented (6/7 languages)
- Fixtures: Complete (7/7 languages)
- Test cases: Complete (14 manifest entries)
- Documentation: Complete (detailed commit messages)

Only remaining work is validation + Ruby scanner completion. This represents
roughly 90% completion of the async error path coverage feature.

Milestone Significance:
=======================

With fixtures and manifest complete, UBS now has:
1. Regression tests for async error detection (buggy fixtures)
2. Precision tests preventing false positives (clean fixtures)
3. Automated validation via manifest runner
4. Clear success criteria (14 passing test cases)

This test-driven approach ensures async error coverage can be maintained
long-term as UBS evolves. Any changes to ast-grep rules, module refactors,
or dependency updates will be validated against these 14 test cases.

Related commits:
- 12d3507: Interpreted language fixtures (JS/Python/Ruby)
- f59787f: Compiled language fixtures (C++/Go/Java/Rust)
- bc45a0c: Manifest test cases (14 entries)

Related issues:
- ultimate_bug_scanner-e3j: Async error path coverage (in progress, ~90% complete)
- ultimate_bug_scanner-d5z: Manifest coverage expansion (in progress)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
